rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Intervenor Century Indemnity Company, As Successor To Cci Insurance Company, As Successor To Insurance Company Of North America, The Potential Insurer Of Defendant James A. Nelson Co., Inc., A Suspended Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Plaintiffs Designation Of The Record Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Plaintiffs Designation Of The Record
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 22, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Intervenor Century Indemnity Company's motion to modify plaintiffs' designation of the record is denied. Plaintiffs elected to proceed using an appendix under CRC 8.124 and Intervenor did not demonstrate good cause to overturn plaintiffs' election. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ***TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY***MELVIN DAVIS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 22, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Insul Company, Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Los Angeles County is granted. No opposition filed. This action is transferred to the County of Los Angeles. However there is no showing that defendant paid the costs and fees for transfer when it filed its motion, and plaintiffs' conditional non-opposition is conditioned on defendant paying the full costs and fees to transfer the case, therefore this action shall not be prosecuted in any court until the required costs and fees are paid. (C.C.P. § 399(a).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
